title: (BUG) cli: contacts.json written with world-readable permissions
description: In cli/src/config.rs Contacts::save() (line 102), the contacts file is written via std::fs::write() which uses the process umask. On systems with a permissive umask (e.g., 0022), contacts.json is created as world-readable (0644), exposing the user's social graph (peer names and pub_id mappings) to any local user on the machine. The identity keyfile uses write_secret_file() with explicit mode 0600 (commands.rs line 107-124). The contacts file should use the same approach. Fix: replace std::fs::write in Contacts::save() with write_secret_file() or equivalent OpenOptions with mode 0600.
